name: Executive Security Cost Leakage Assessment
description: Executive Assurance Gap and Security Cost Leakage Index preliminary decision-support assessment. Not an audit finding.

inputs.label: Organisation name
inputs.guidance: Enter organisation or business unit name

inputs.label: Industry vertical
inputs.guidance: Select closest industry

inputs.label: Total sites / facilities
inputs.guidance: Count all sites covered by security arrangements

inputs.label: Guard force total
inputs.guidance: Total contracted guards/officers across all sites and shifts

inputs.label: Annual security contract value incl. ad hoc services
inputs.guidance: Include guarding, extra services, variations and recurring technology service costs

inputs.label: Estimated annual security losses – low
inputs.guidance: Conservative range for losses, claims, theft, shrinkage or avoidable incidents

inputs.label: Estimated annual security losses – high
inputs.guidance: Upper range for losses, claims, theft, shrinkage or avoidable incidents

inputs.label: Internal security team size
inputs.guidance: Full-time internal staff responsible for security management and governance

inputs.label: Number of contracted security vendors
inputs.guidance: Guarding, monitoring, technology and specialist providers

inputs.label: Integrated technology footprint coverage %
inputs.guidance: Share of geographic footprint covered by integrated surveillance/access/patrol monitoring

inputs.label: Technology used for independent SLA verification %
inputs.guidance: Extent to which security technology is actively used to verify SLA obligations

inputs.label: Manual security records for service verification %
inputs.guidance: Share of service verification dependent on manual registers/OBs/paper records

inputs.label: Centralised monitored surveillance coverage %
inputs.guidance: Sites where surveillance materially supports access/patrol/incident verification

inputs.label: Electronic access control coverage %
inputs.guidance: Sites with usable electronic access control evidence

inputs.label: Real-time patrol monitoring coverage %
inputs.guidance: Sites where patrol monitoring is available and reviewed in real time

inputs.label: Delayed patrol reporting %
inputs.guidance: Patrol data downloaded/reviewed after shift or monthly

inputs.label: Proof of supervisory/management visits %
inputs.guidance: Independent proof quality of supervisory and management site visits

inputs.label: Proof of guard attendance verification %
inputs.guidance: Independent proof quality of guard attendance verification

inputs.label: Performance allowance paid
inputs.guidance: YES/NO

inputs.label: Night allowance paid
inputs.guidance: YES/NO

inputs.label: Firearm allowance paid
inputs.guidance: YES/NO

inputs.label: Site allowance paid
inputs.guidance: YES/NO

inputs.label: National Key Point allowance paid
inputs.guidance: YES/NO

questions.text: Do you have a Service Level Agreement as part of the security service agreement?
questions.evidenceHint: Signed security service agreement and SLA schedules.

questions.text: If the Board asked tomorrow, could management certify that every contracted security service was independently verified over the past 12 months?
questions.evidenceHint: Board packs, assurance certificates and independent verification records.

questions.text: How much of your annual security expenditure is independently verified rather than accepted through supplier reporting?
questions.evidenceHint: Sampling reports, verification dashboards or reconciliations.

questions.text: If your largest guarding contract was audited tomorrow, what percentage of contractual obligations could be supported by independently verified evidence?
questions.evidenceHint: Contract-obligation evidence matrix and supporting records.

questions.text: How much money did the organisation recover through SLA penalties or service credits during the past financial year?
questions.evidenceHint: Penalty registers, credit notes and service-credit reconciliations.

options.label: Less than 1% of annual contract value
More than 3% of annual contract value

questions.text: Has the security deployment model been independently validated against current operational risk during the past 24 months?
questions.evidenceHint: Independent deployment review or risk-based guarding model.

questions.text: How many guard posts currently exist mainly because they have always existed?
questions.evidenceHint: Post establishment list and current risk justification.

questions.text: How reliant is your organisation on manual security records for service verification?
questions.evidenceHint: Occurrence books, registers and electronic verification records.

questions.text: If security enabling technology is integrated, how much of the geographical footprint is effectively covered?
questions.evidenceHint: Technology coverage map by site or region.

questions.text: To what extent is security enabling technology actively used for independent verification of SLA obligations?
questions.evidenceHint: SLA verification reports produced from security technology.

questions.text: Do executive reports include an independent assurance score or evidence-based performance view rather than only operational activity?
questions.evidenceHint: Executive reports, scorecards and assurance dashboards.

questions.text: Are security incidents and losses reconciled against SLA performance, contractor obligations and potential penalties?
questions.evidenceHint: Incident, loss, SLA and penalty reconciliation records.

questions.text: Are ad hoc additional security services formally approved, tracked and reconciled against contract terms?
questions.evidenceHint: Ad hoc service approvals, invoices and reconciliation schedules.

questions.text: Are supplier reports independently sampled or validated every month across a representative site set?
questions.evidenceHint: Monthly supplier-report sampling packs.

questions.text: Is supplier non-performance linked to formal consequence management in monthly or quarterly governance forums?
questions.evidenceHint: Governance minutes and consequence-management records.

questions.text: Does your PMIS / technology stack cover the entire organisation sufficiently to support independent service verification?
questions.evidenceHint: PMIS coverage report and organisation-wide integration map.

questions.text: Can executive management quantify the financial value created by the current security programme?
questions.evidenceHint: Benefits, avoided-loss and value-realisation reports.

questions.text: Is the internal security function adequately resourced to independently verify SLA performance nationally?
questions.evidenceHint: Organisation chart, role profiles and oversight workload.

questions.text: How frequently are high-risk sites subjected to independent assurance review?
questions.evidenceHint: Independent high-risk site assurance review schedule and reports.

questions.text: Were SLA penalties identified and applied during the last 12 months where performance failures occurred?
questions.evidenceHint: Penalty calculations, approvals and proof of application.

assumptions.label: Minimum leakage base floor
assumptions.description: Conservative leakage floor present in large outsourced guarding environments.

assumptions.label: Minimum leakage | Manual record weight
assumptions.description: Higher manual dependence raises unverifiable service delivery risk.

assumptions.label: Minimum leakage | Delayed patrol reporting weight
assumptions.description: After-shift or monthly review weakens recoverability and timely correction.

assumptions.label: Minimum leakage | Supervisory proof gap weight
assumptions.description: Lower proof of supervisory visits raises baseline leakage risk.

assumptions.label: Minimum leakage | Attendance proof gap weight
assumptions.description: Lower proof of attendance verification raises baseline leakage risk.

assumptions.label: Minimum leakage | Internal capacity gap weight
assumptions.description: Insufficient internal governance headcount raises baseline risk.

assumptions.label: Minimum leakage | Scale complexity weight
assumptions.description: Guard force, spend, premises count, and footprint complexity create coordination risk.

assumptions.label: Minimum leakage | Allowance complexity weight
assumptions.description: More active allowance types raise baseline validation complexity.

assumptions.label: Likely reality | Surveillance gap weight
assumptions.description: Limited monitored surveillance coverage reduces proof quality.

assumptions.label: Likely reality | Access control gap weight
assumptions.description: Limited central access control coverage reduces certainty.

assumptions.label: Likely reality | Electronic record monitoring gap weight
assumptions.description: Electronic data not centrally monitored loses much of its value.

assumptions.label: Likely reality | Real-time patrol gap weight
assumptions.description: Missing real-time patrol visibility increases variance between invoice and delivery.

assumptions.label: Likely reality | Manual record weight
assumptions.description: Manual records further widen likely leakage above the minimum band.

assumptions.label: Likely reality | Scale complexity weight
assumptions.description: Complex estate sizes widen likely leakage.

assumptions.label: Likely reality | Allowance complexity weight
assumptions.description: Allowance admin complexity increases likely leakage.

assumptions.label: Maximum exposure | Manual record weight
assumptions.description: Worst-case slippage is highest where proof is manual.

assumptions.label: Maximum exposure | Delayed patrol reporting weight
assumptions.description: Delayed reporting creates broad exposure.

assumptions.label: Maximum exposure | Supervisory proof gap weight
assumptions.description: Weak supervisory proof expands loss envelope.

assumptions.label: Maximum exposure | Attendance proof gap weight
assumptions.description: Weak attendance proof expands loss envelope.

assumptions.label: Maximum exposure | Surveillance gap weight
assumptions.description: Low surveillance verification expands exposure.

assumptions.label: Maximum exposure | Electronic record monitoring gap weight
assumptions.description: Electronic data not watched centrally provides weak defensibility.

assumptions.label: Maximum exposure | Internal capacity gap weight
assumptions.description: Low governance staffing expands maximum loss envelope.

assumptions.label: Maximum exposure | Allowance complexity weight
assumptions.description: Allowance complexity widens exposure in the upper band.

assumptions.label: Guard force saturation point
assumptions.description: At or above this scale the model assumes full guard-force complexity.

assumptions.label: Annual cost saturation point
assumptions.description: At or above this spend the model assumes full spend complexity.

assumptions.label: Protected premises saturation point
assumptions.description: At or above this estate size the model assumes full site complexity.

assumptions.label: Geographical footprint saturation point
assumptions.description: At or above 9 provinces or regions the model assumes full footprint complexity.

assumptions.label: Target sites per internal security staff member
assumptions.description: One internal oversight person per this number of protected premises.

assumptions.label: Minimum leakage cap
assumptions.description: Hard cap for conservative band.

assumptions.label: Likely leakage cap
assumptions.description: Hard cap for likely band.

assumptions.label: Maximum exposure cap
assumptions.description: Hard cap for maximum exposure band.

assumptions.label: Recoverable low factor
assumptions.description: Conservative recoverable portion of minimum leakage.

assumptions.label: Recoverable high factor
assumptions.description: Conservative recoverable portion of likely leakage.

recommendationRules.title: Contract and SLA remediation review
recommendationRules.summary: Establish a comprehensive, measurable SLA with evidence requirements, service credits and governance controls.
recommendationRules.serviceOffering: Independent Contract and SLA Assurance Review

recommendationRules.title: Independent service-verification programme
recommendationRules.summary: Introduce independent sampling and reconciliation of contracted service delivery across representative sites.

recommendationRules.title: Contract-obligation evidence matrix
recommendationRules.summary: Map every material contractual obligation to an evidence source, owner, frequency and exception process.

recommendationRules.title: Electronic service-verification roadmap
recommendationRules.summary: Reduce dependence on manual records by integrating attendance, patrol, access and incident evidence.

recommendationRules.title: Representative monthly assurance sampling
recommendationRules.summary: Implement independent monthly sampling across high-risk and representative sites.

recommendationRules.title: Penalty and service-credit recovery review
recommendationRules.summary: Reconcile service failures to contractual remedies and establish formal approval and recovery workflows.

recommendationRules.title: Security governance operating-model review
recommendationRules.summary: Align internal assurance capacity, roles and review frequencies to estate scale and supplier complexity.

recommendationRules.title: Security value and benefits framework
recommendationRules.summary: Create a repeatable model for avoided loss, recovered value, service credits and assurance outcomes.

recommendationRules.title: Risk-based guarding deployment optimisation
recommendationRules.summary: Independently validate posts, shifts and staffing against current risk and technology coverage.
